title: Winner of iF Design Award 2018
excerpt: We are excited to announce that the Green Science series by 4M Creative Educational Toys has received the world-renowned iF Design Award 2018.
content:
We are excited to announce that the Green Science series by 4M Creative Educational Toys has received the world-renowned iF Design Award 2018. The honour was awarded by International Design Forum, a prestigious independent design institute in Germany founded in 1954.

Together with the Red Dot Award in Germany and IDEA Design Award from the United States, the iF Design Awards is regarded as the Oscars in the world of design excellence. Comprising of 63 independent design experts from 20 countries, this year, the iF Design Award judging panel assessed over 6,400 pieces of submissions from over 54 countries.

Our Green Science series has been selected for their innovative concepts of incorporating environmental awareness into toy designs, and cultivating children's sense of social responsibility through play.

Since its launch in 2007, 4M Green Science series has collected major international accolades including the Red Dot Design Award (Germany), A' Design Gold Award (Italy), China Good Design, DFA Design for Asia Awards and the Hong Kong Awards for Industries. The addition of the iF Design Award further affirms 4M's commitment to designing STEAM toys through creativity and innovation.

The award presentation ceremony was hold in Munich, Germany on 9th March 2018. All the awarded products were exhibited at the iF Design Exhibition in Hamburg.

Once again, we would like to thank our design team and our business partners for their continuous support and partnership which motivate us to do better in educating the young minds.

For more information about iF Design Award, please visit https://ifworlddesignguide.com/design-excellence.

title: 4M Maglev Train Wins Best New S.T.E.M Toy
excerpt: Maglev Train Model, our most recent addition to the successful Green Science Eco-Engineering series is an instant hit, winning Best New Toy in the newly created S.T.E.M. category at Toy Fair 2017 in London.
content:
Maglev Train Model, our most recent addition to the successful Green Science Eco-Engineering series is an instant hit, winning Best New Toy in the newly created S.T.E.M. category at Toy Fair 2017 in London.

Maglev, or Magnetic Levitation, works on the same principle as modern Superfast Trains, using opposing magnets to levitate the body of the train to create a smooth, frictionless, energy efficient transportation system, 100% emission-free.

Easy to assemble and use, Eco-Engineering Maglev Train Model, clearly demonstrates the efficient, clean power of magnetism, inspiring children with the possibilities of sustainable green technology.

The U.K.'s Toy Fair, organized by the British Toy and Hobby Association (BTHA), is one of the first major toys shows of the year, and a clear indication of global trends. One such trend that continues to gain momentum is S.T.E.M. integrated educational toys, and the addition of the new S.T.E.M. category shows perhaps it is more than a trend.

Natasha Crookes of the BTHA remarks, "As the market evolves, it is fantastic to see the judging panel introduce new categories such as the S.T.E.M category which recognises how important toys and the value of play are to a child's development."

We applaud the BTHA, and we look forward to winning even more STEM and design awards around the world in 2017. However, we are focused beyond STEM. We believe that it is creativity and artistic imagination that lead to true innovation and progress, as well as fantastic works of beauty, hence S.T.E.A.M.

At 4M, we have been incorporating core principles of science, technology, engineering, arts & creativity and mathematics into every one of our products long before S.T.E.M or S.T.E.A.M were recognised as essential aspects of modern educational development.

We are proud to have been early S.T.E.A.M. innovators, and we are even more delighted to promote the ideas of learning-as-play, science experienced through arts and crafts, and imaginative wonder through toys that are Creative, Innovative, Inspiring and Fun.

## Green Science Eco-Engineering – A Worldwide Smash Hit Series

Maglev Train Model is the latest product to win accolades in our Green Science series, which has been praised by critics and parents since Tin Can Robot was launched in 2007.

Green Science was recognised and honoured in 2015 with the Oscar Award of design; a prestigious Red Dot Design Award, and again in 2016 with a Gold A' Design Award, raising our international profile and further enhancing our reputation as a leading innovator in educational toys.

Each year we continue to innovate and incorporate the latest technology with cool designs and fun applications, coming out with new educational play concepts, revamping and improving top-sellers, and leading the market in S.T.E.A.M. concepts.

We look forward to an exciting and successful 2017, the year of STE4M.

title: 4M Wins Triple Honours at HKAI 2016
excerpt: AR Wonder: Dinosaur DNA has proven to be one of the biggest hits in 2016, most recently winning in three categories at the HKAI; including Consumer Product Design, Innovation and Creativity, and Technological Achievement.
content:
AR Wonder: Dinosaur DNA has proven to be one of the biggest hits in 2016, most recently winning in three categories at the HKAI; including Consumer Product Design, Innovation and Creativity, and Technological Achievement.

The HKAI awards encompass seven product categories, and we are proud to say that 4M's AR Wonder: Dinosaur DNA is the first product to ever win in three HKAI categories in a single year; an unprecedented honour for an unprecedented toy.

Launched in the spring of 2016, AR Wonder: Dinosaur DNA combines our popular Dig-a-Dinosaur series with Augmented Reality technology. Children excavate and assemble their dinosaur skeleton, then scan special DNA Stickers into their smartphone or device through the Dino Codes App, and, like 21st century magic, the dinosaur comes to life on-screen in beautifully rendered detail.

The Dino Codes App is social media friendly, allowing for easy sharing of photos and video with other dinosaur enthusiasts, as well as containing loads of interesting dino-facts.

By incorporating AR technology with traditional hands-on excavation and assembly, Dinosaur DNA engages children's minds on multiple levels within the STEAM (science, technology, engineering, arts and math) framework.

AR Wonder: Dinosaur DNA was an instant international success with industry delegates and media. As one of the hottest toys released in recent years, it quickly garnered two prestigious industry awards; a Silver A' from A' Design Award & Competition, and an elusive Gold Award from the Hong Kong Smart Gifts Design Awards.

Critics, industry experts, and kids agree: AR Wonder: Dinosaur DNA is innovative, creative, inspiring, and fun!

Even as AR Wonder: Dinosaur DNA continues to impress and gain accolades, we are getting ready for an exciting 2017 full of interactive, high-tech iToys and new toys integrating STEAM concepts.

title: KidzLabs Series has received the Consumer Product Design Award 2015
excerpt: 2015 has been a wonderful year for 4M. Our signiture KidzLabs Series has received the Consumer Product Design Award 2015 from Hong Kong Awards for Industries on 13 Dec 2015.
content:
2015 has been a wonderful year for 4M. Our signiture KidzLabs Series has received the Consumer Product Design Award 2015 from Hong Kong Awards for Industries on 13 Dec 2015.

KidzLabs began in 1999 with the idea that young children love making discoveries about the world around them. We introduce children to the wonders of mechanics, electronics, optics, astronomy, chemistry, paleontology and much more.

Today KidzLabs is among the world's most successful educational toy series, recognized and recommended by institues, parents and educators, and receiving numerous accolades and awards from reputable parental and consumer organizations in many different countries.

For more information about the award, please visit website: www.hkindustryaward.org/en/index.html

title: Add a Red Dot on Green Science 4M Wins a World-Class Design Award
excerpt: We are proud to announce that our Green Science series has just won the internationally respected Red Dot Award for Product Design 2015.
content:
We are proud to announce that our Green Science series has just won the internationally respected Red Dot Award for Product Design 2015.

Now in its 60th year, the world-class design competition attracted 4,928 entries, submitted by 1,994 participants from 56 countries. Described by the jury as displaying "uncompromising competency in development", our Green Science series – which now comprises more than 20 creative products – received high marks for innovation, functionality, ecological compatibility and other evaluation criteria, deservedly winning the sought-after Red Dot seal of quality.

Since its creation in 2007, our signature Green Science series has received over 100 prestigious design and product awards from around the world, including Hong Kong Awards for Industries, Design for Asia Award, the Best Green Toy Award, Toy of the Year Award in numerous countries, and other individual product awards presented by the trade.

As one of the most renowned design awards in the world, the Red Dot Award is a confirmation of our standing in the toy trade, and the success of this series.

The award will be presented at the Red Dot Gala in Essen, Germany, on 29 June, at a glamorous ceremony attended by more than 1,200 guests and hosted by its founder, Professor Dr Peter Zee in Essen's Aalto-Theater. The winning products will then be on display for four weeks in the special exhibition "Design on Stage" before being included as permanent exhibit in the prestigious Red Dot Design Museum.

This is a victory we could not have achieved without the support and dedication of our TE4M, partners and stakeholders. We are proud to be able to bring this win home, and will strive to push 4M to even greater heights in the future.

More information of Red Dot is available at www.red-dot.org/press
